seo.title: Expert Site Reliability Engineer Resume Example & ATS Templates 2026
seo.description: Optimize your Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) resume with our expert guide, examples, and ATS-friendly templates to increase your chances of landing an interview.
seo.keywords: Site Reliability Engineer resume, SRE resume example, SRE CV template, pass ATS resume, infrastructure engineer cv
seo.ogTitle: How to Write a Site Reliability Engineer (SRE) Resume in 2026
seo.ogDescription: Use our verified SRE resume example and template optimized to pass applicant tracking systems.

hero.title: Site Reliability Engineer Resume Examples
hero.description: Stop getting auto-rejected by ATS screening bots. Here is the exact SRE resume structure, keywords, certifications, and action verbs you need to land interviews in 2026.

mistakes: Using non-standard, complex multi-column layouts that confuse ATS parsers.
Listing technologies (e.g. 'Kubernetes') without describing practical scaling or deployment impact.
Failing to quantify achievements (e.g., writing 'Fixed system outages' instead of 'Reduced MTTR by 35% through automated remediation scripts').
Omitting critical systems keywords like observability, latency, error budgets, or post-mortem analysis.
Submitting resumes with text boxes, graphs, icons, or logos that break parser syntax.
tips: Structure your experience bullets using the STAR method, starting with action verbs like automated, architected, or optimized.
Clearly highlight your programming skills (Python/Go) alongside operations/cloud platforms.
Ensure your cloud certifications (CKA, AWS DevOps Professional) are listed clearly for screeners to see.
Save and submit your resume in PDF format to preserve layout across all devices.

exampleResume.name: David Chen
exampleResume.title: Site Reliability Engineer
exampleResume.summary: Systems-minded software engineer with 4+ years of experience specializing in SRE, container orchestration, and Infrastructure as Code. Proven track record of reducing system downtime by 40% and deploying automated self-healing configurations on AWS.

bullets: Architected and managed production Kubernetes clusters supporting 20+ microservices, improving deployment speed by 45%.
Provisioned AWS infrastructure using Terraform, reducing resource provisioning time from days to minutes.
Implemented Prometheus and Grafana dashboards for observability, reducing alert noise by 50% and MTTR by 30%.
Developed automated log rotation and backup scripts in Python and Bash, saving 8 hours of manual maintenance weekly.
Maintained Jenkins CI/CD deployment pipelines, ensuring 99.9% build success rate across staging and production.

exampleResume.skills: Go, Python, Bash, Kubernetes, Docker, Terraform, Prometheus, Grafana, AWS, Linux internals, Git

projects.name: Automated Chaos Testing Suite
projects.description: Built a custom chaos engineering tool in Go to inject network latency and pod failures, validating system self-healing capabilities.

projects.name: GitOps Deployment Pipeline
projects.description: Designed and deployed a declarative GitOps pipeline using ArgoCD to synchronize Kubernetes state with Git repositories.

extendedContent.heading: Detailed Professional Summary for SRE
extendedContent.content: A well-crafted professional summary is essential to catch the recruiter's eye within seconds. For SREs, it's crucial to balance coding competence with systems operational expertise. Instead of just listing tools, write about how you leverage them to drive reliability and efficiency. Mention years of experience, specific cloud platforms, and metrics like availability percentage or MTTR improvements.

extendedContent.heading: Why This Resume Works
extendedContent.content: This SRE resume template works because it is structured in a clean, single-column format that guarantees 100% parseability by ATS systems. It uses standard section headers, highlights industry-standard keywords like Kubernetes and Terraform, and quantifies achievements in the experience section to prove business value.

extendedContent.heading: Recruiter Insights: What Infrastructure Managers Look For
extendedContent.content: Hiring managers look for SREs who treat operations as a software problem. They want to see automation first, systems-level problem solving, and a blameless post-mortem mindset. Possessing cloud-native certifications (like the CKA) and a clean GitHub portfolio containing Terraform or scripting projects provides a significant hiring advantage.

faq.q: What is the best layout for an SRE resume?
faq.a: A clean, single-column chronological layout. Avoid tables, columns, graphics, or non-standard fonts to ensure ATS systems parse it correctly.

faq.q: Should I list soft skills on my SRE resume?
faq.a: Yes, but demonstrate them through achievements, such as leading incident response or writing post-mortems, rather than just listing them.

faq.q: How long should my SRE resume be?
faq.a: Keep it to one page if you have less than 7-10 years of experience, and a maximum of two pages for senior roles.

faq.q: How do I optimize my resume for ATS?
faq.a: Incorporate key terms from the job description (like Kubernetes, IaC, CI/CD) naturally in your experience bullet points.

faq.q: Is coding experience required on an SRE resume?
faq.a: Yes. SREs are expected to write code for automation. Highlight languages like Go, Python, or Bash prominently.

faq.q: Should I link my GitHub on my resume?
faq.a: Yes. A link to your GitHub containing clean Terraform modules, custom scripts, or Go utilities is highly recommended.

faq.q: Are certifications useful on an SRE resume?
faq.a: Yes, they help bypass automated screens and prove baseline proficiency to recruiters.

faq.q: What is the STAR method for resume bullets?
faq.a: STAR stands for Situation, Task, Action, and Result. Describe the problem, your action, and the quantifiable outcome.

faq.q: How far back should my SRE experience go?
faq.a: Only list the last 10-15 years of relevant technical work history.

faq.q: Should I include a photo on my SRE resume?
faq.a: No, omit photos to avoid bias and meet standard US/UK recruitment guidelines.
